steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a sentence boundary question. The blank falls between two independent clauses: “In 2017 … Nutcracker” and “According to reviewers … Latin American culture.” The correct choice must properly separate those two complete sentences without creating a run-on or comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nutcracker according,: Creates a run-on sentence by fusing two independent clauses with no boundary punctuation between them; the comma after “according” incorrectly splits the phrase “according to reviewers.” ✗
  • Choice B — Nutcracker, according: Creates a comma splice. A comma alone cannot separate two independent clauses; a coordinating conjunction would also be needed. ✗
  • Choice C — Nutcracker according: Creates a run-on sentence by fusing two independent clauses with no punctuation or conjunction at all. ✗
  • Choice D — Nutcracker. According: Uses a period to correctly end the first independent clause and begin a new sentence. “According to reviewers” then functions as a properly placed introductory phrase for the second sentence. ✓
D is correct.
